Test Description: Approve a pending user and verify email sent
Steps:
1. Go to /admin → Users tab
2. Find a pending user (e.g. eneazyraider@gmail.com)
3. Click Approve
Expected Result: User receives approval email at their address

Test Description: Reject a pending user with reason
Steps:
1. Go to /admin → Users tab
2. Find a pending user
3. Click Reject → enter a reason → confirm
Expected Result: User receives rejection email with the reason

Steps:
1. Go to /admin → Users tab
2. Find a pending PARTNER user
3. Check the Approve button
Expected Result: Approve button should be DISABLED with warning about missing references

Steps:
1. On the same pending partner user
2. Click 'Bypass' button → enter justification → confirm
3. Check Approve button is now enabled
4. Click Approve
Expected Result: Partner receives bypass approval email AND account approval email

Test Description: Change exposition status and verify email
Steps:
1. Go to /admin → Expositions tab
2. Click eye icon on a request
3. Change status to Approved (or Invoice Sent / Paid)
Expected Result: Requester receives email matching the status change

Steps:
1. Go to /admin → Sponsorships tab
2. Click eye icon on a request
3. Mark as Invoice Sent → then Paid → then Approve
Expected Result: Email sent at each step; org tier updates on final approval

Steps:
1. Go to /admin → Webinar Requests tab
2. Select a submitted request
3. Change status to 'accepted'
Expected Result: Proposer receives acceptance email; event auto-created

Steps:
1. Go to /admin → Webinar Requests tab
2. Select a submitted request
3. Change status to 'rejected'
Expected Result: Proposer receives rejection email

Steps:
1. Go to /admin → RFPs tab
2. Click 'Close' on an open RFP
Expected Result: Marina owner receives 'RFP closed' email

Steps:
1. Go to /admin → Consultations tab
2. Click 'Close' on an open consultation
Expected Result: Marina owner receives 'consultation closed' email

Steps:
1. Go to /admin → Webinar Requests tab
2. Find a submitted proposal matching your sectors
3. Click Pre-approve
Expected Result: Admin (info@m3monaco.com) receives email about moderator pre-approval

Test Description: Submit an RFP and verify admin notification
Steps:
1. Go to /submit-rfp
2. Fill in title and scope and sector
3. Submit
Expected Result: Admin receives 'New RFP' notification email

Test Description: Submit a consultation and verify admin notification
Steps:
1. Go to /submit-consultation
2. Fill in title and description
3. Submit
Expected Result: Admin receives 'New Consultation' notification email

Steps:
1. Go to /events
2. Click on an event → click Register
3. Complete registration
Expected Result: You receive event registration confirmation email

Steps:
1. Go to /network (Marketplace)
2. Find a marina
3. Click Contact → write a message → send
Expected Result: Marina user receives 'new contact request' email

Steps:
1. Go to /request-webinar
2. Fill in title / description / language / sectors
3. Submit
Expected Result: Admin receives 'New Webinar Proposal' notification email

Steps:
1. Go to /account → References tab
2. Scroll to bypass section
3. Fill reason + company background → submit
Expected Result: Bypass request appears in admin panel (Users → partner detail)

Login As: Any new account or reset onboarding
Steps:
1. Switch language to French (navbar)
2. Go to /onboarding
3. Go through persona selection and marina form
Expected Result: All labels / headings / buttons should be in French. Note any English text remaining

Steps:
1. Switch language to French
2. Go to /account → Pre-Audit tab
3. Check all headings / buttons / scoring labels
Expected Result: All text should be in French. Note any English text remaining

Test Description: Check English on onboarding still works
Steps:
1. Switch language to English
2. Go to /onboarding
3. Check all form labels and buttons
Expected Result: Everything displays correctly in English (no broken t() keys showing)

Steps:
1. Go to /admin → any tab with table (Users / Events / RFPs)
2. Right-click an icon button (eye icon) → Inspect
3. Check for aria-label attribute
Expected Result: All icon-only buttons should have aria-label (e.g. 'View details')

Steps:
1. Open browser DevTools → Network tab
2. Load https://connect.m3monaco.com
3. Check that multiple JS chunks load (vendor-react / vendor-ui / vendor-supabase / etc.)
Expected Result: Multiple smaller JS files instead of one huge file

Steps:
1. Open DevTools → Console tab
2. Navigate through several pages (home / resources / events / admin)
3. Note any console.error or console.warn messages
Expected Result: No errors/warnings in production (all wrapped in DEV checks)

Steps:
1. Open DevTools → Network tab
2. Load the site → click on the main document request
3. Check Response Headers
Expected Result: Should see: X-Frame-Options: DENY / X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff / Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin

Steps:
1. Login with any account
2. Open console → check for errors mentioning marina_profiles / partner_profiles / media_partner_profiles
Expected Result: No references to legacy tables; profile loads correctly
